concepts.title: Dielectric Medium Between Capacitor Plates
concepts.conceptBody:
Dielectric Medium Between Capacitor Plates

Michel Faraday found that when certain non-conducting materials such as glass, paper or plastic are introduced between the plates of a capacitor, its capacity increases. These materials are called dielectrics and the ratio of capacity of a capacitor when completely filled with dielectric $\text{C}$ to that without dielectric ${{\text{C}}_{0}}$ is called dielectric constant $\text{K}$, relative permitivity ${{\varepsilon }_{\text{r}}}=\frac{\text{C}}{{{\text{C}}_{0}}}$

Note : Regarding dielectrics, it is worth nothing that:

(i) These are non-conductors upto a certain value of field depending on its nature. If the field exceeds this limiting value called dielectric-strength, dielectric lose its insulating property and begins to conduct.

(ii) These have either permanent dipole moment (polar-dielectrics, e.g., water) or acquire induced dipole moment (non-polar dielectrics) when placed in an electric field.

(iii) The dielectric constant of polar dielectric depends on its temperature and due to thermal agitation with rise in temperature decreases.

The effect of dielectric on other physical quantities such as charge, PD, field and energy associated with a capacitor depends on the fact that whether the charged capacitor is isolated (i.e., charge is held constant).

So if ${{\text{q}}_{0}},{{\text{C}}_{0}},\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{V}}_{0}},{{\text{E}}_{0}}$ and ${{\text{U}}_{0}}$ represent the charge, capacity, $\text{PD}$, electric field and energy associated with charged air capacitor respectively, with introduction of a dielectric slab of dielectric constant $\text{K}$ between the plates.

(a) If charge is held constant, i.e., battery disconnected 

(1) Charge remains unchanged, i.e., $q={{q}_{0}}$, as in isolated system charge is conserved.

(2) Capacity increases, i.e., $\text{C}=\text{K}{{\text{C}}_{0}}$, as by presence of a dielectric capacity becomes $\text{K}$ times.

(3) $\text{PD}$ between the plates decreases, i.e., $\text{V}=\left( {{\text{V}}_{0}}/\text{K} \right)$ as $\text{V}=\frac{\text{q}}{\text{C}}=\frac{{{\text{q}}_{0}}}{\text{K}{{\text{C}}_{0}}}$ [as $\text{q}={{\text{q}}_{0}}$ and $\text{C}=\text{K}{{\text{C}}_{0}}$ ]

(4) Field between the plates decreases, i.e. $\text{E}=\left( {{\text{E}}_{0}}/\text{K} \right)$, as

$\text{E}=\frac{\text{V}}{\text{d}}=\frac{{{\text{V}}_{0}}}{\text{Kd}}=\frac{{{\text{E}}_{0}}}{\text{ K}}\left[ \text{as }\text{ V}=\frac{{{\text{V}}_{0}}}{\text{ K}}\text{ and }{{\text{E}}_{0}}=\frac{{{\text{V}}_{0}}}{\text{ d}} \right]$

(5) Energy stored in the capacitor decreases, i.e., $\text{U}=\left( {{\text{U}}_{0}}/\text{K} \right)$, as

$\text{U}=\frac{{{\text{q}}^{2}}}{2\text{C}}=\frac{\text{q}_{0}^{2}}{2\text{K}{{\text{C}}_{0}}}=\frac{{{\text{U}}_{0}}}{\text{ K}}\left[ {} \right.$as $\text{q}={{\text{q}}_{0}}$ and $\left. \text{C}=\text{K}{{\text{C}}_{0}} \right]$

(b) If potential is held constant, i.e. battery remains attached 

(1) $\text{PD}$ remains constant, i.e., $\text{V}={{\text{V}}_{0}}$, as battery is a source of constant potential difference.

(2) Capacity increases, i.e., $\text{C}=\text{K}{{\text{C}}_{0}}$, as by presence of a dielectric capacity becomes $\text{K}$ times.

(3) Charge on capacitor increases, i.e. $\text{q}=\text{K}{{\text{q}}_{0}}$ as, $\text{q}=\text{CV}=\left( \text{K}{{\text{C}}_{0}} \right)\text{V}=\text{K}{{\text{q}}_{0}}$ as $\left. {{\text{q}}_{0}}=\text{C}{{\text{V}}_{0}} \right\}$

(4) Electric field remains unchanged, i.e., $\text{E}={{\text{E}}_{0}}$ as,

$\text{E}=\left[ \frac{\text{V}}{\text{d}} \right]=\frac{{{\text{V}}_{0}}}{\text{ d}}={{\text{E}}_{0}}\left[ {} \right.$as $\text{ V}={{\text{V}}_{0}}$ and $\left. \frac{{{\text{V}}_{0}}}{\text{ d}}={{\text{E}}_{0}} \right]$

(5) Energy stored in the capacitor increases, i.e., $\text{U}=\text{K}{{\text{U}}_{0}}$ as, $\text{U}=\frac{1}{2}\text{C}{{\text{V}}^{2}}=\frac{1}{2}\left( \text{K}{{\text{C}}_{0}} \right){{\left( {{\text{V}}_{0}} \right)}^{2}}$

$=\frac{1}{2}\text{K}{{\text{U}}_{0}}\left[ \text{as }\text{ C}=\text{K}{{\text{C}}_{0}}\text{ and }{{\text{U}}_{0}}=\frac{1}{2}{{\text{C}}_{0}}\text{ V}_{0}^{2} \right]$

Note : While solving problems of this type proceed in the following steps :

(i) If battery is disconnected $\text{{q}'}=\text{q}$ and if battery remains attached $\text{{V}'}=\text{V}$

(ii) For a capacitor as $\text{C}=\left( {{\varepsilon }_{0}}\text{KA}/\text{d} \right)$, find whether $\text{{C}'}$ is greater or lesser than $\text{C}$ in accordance with given problem (as $\text{C}$ depends on $\text{K},\text{A}$ and $\text{d}$ only)

(iii) Now using $\text{{q}'}=\text{{C}'{V}'}$ and points (1) and (2), find $\text{{q}'}$ if $\text{{V}'}$ is given and $\text{{V}'}$ if $\text{{q}'}$ is given.

(iv) To calculate $\text{{E}'}$ use the formula :

$\text{{E}'}=\frac{{\text{{V}'}}}{{\text{{d}'}}}$ or $\frac{{\text{{q}'}}}{\text{AK}{{\varepsilon }_{0}}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left[ {} \right.$ as $\left. \text{{E}'}=\frac{{{\sigma }'}}{\varepsilon } \right]$ as the case may be

(v) To calculate ${U}'$ use the formula : ${U}'=\frac{1}{2}{q}'{V}'=\frac{1}{2}{C}'{{\left( {{V}'} \right)}^{2}}={{\left( {{q}'} \right)}^{2}}/2{C}'$ as the case may be

(vi) To calculate $\text{W}$ apply conservation of energy i.e., ${{\text{W}}_{\text{IF}}}=\left( {{\text{U}}_{\text{F}}}-{{\text{U}}_{\text{I}}} \right)$

concepts.title: Multiple Dielectric Medium Between Capacitor Plates
concepts.conceptBody:
Multiple Dielectric Medium Between Capacitor Plates

(I) A parallel plate capacitor contains two dielectric slabs of thickness ${{\text{d}}_{1}},\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{d}}_{2}}$ and dielectric constants ${{\text{k}}_{1}}\And {{\text{k}}_{2}}$ respectively. The area of the capacitor plates and slabs is equal to A.

Considering the capacitor as a combination of two capacitors in series, the equivalent capacitance $\text{C}$ is given by :

$\begin{array}{*{35}{r}} {} & \frac{1}{\text{C}}=\frac{1}{{{\text{C}}_{1}}}+\frac{1}{{{\text{C}}_{2}}} \\ {} & \frac{1}{\text{C}}=\frac{{{\text{d}}_{1}}}{{{\text{k}}_{1}}{{\in }_{0}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ A}}+\frac{{{\text{d}}_{2}}}{{{\text{k}}_{2}}{{\in }_{0}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ A}} \\ {} & \text{C}=\frac{{{\in }_{0}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ A}}{\frac{{{\text{d}}_{1}}}{{{\text{k}}_{1}}}+\frac{{{\text{d}}_{2}}}{{{\text{k}}_{2}}}} \\ \end{array}$

In general for more than one dielectric slab :

If $\text{V}$ is the potential difference across the plates, the electric fields in the dielectrics are given as :

$\begin{array}{*{35}{r}} {} & {{\text{E}}_{1}}=\frac{{{\text{V}}_{1}}}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{d}}_{1}}}=\frac{\text{Q}}{{{\text{C}}_{1}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{d}}_{1}}}=\frac{\text{CV}}{{{\text{C}}_{1}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{d}}_{1}}} \\ {} & {{\text{E}}_{1}}=\frac{1}{\frac{{{\text{d}}_{1}}}{{{\text{k}}_{1}}}+\frac{{{\text{d}}_{2}}}{{{\text{k}}_{2}}}}\left( \frac{\text{V}}{{{\text{k}}_{1}}} \right) \\ \end{array}$

The equivalent capacitance is not affected by changing the distance of slab from the parallel Plates.

If the slab is of metal the equivalent capacitance is :

$\text{C}=\frac{{{\in }_{0}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ A}}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ d}-\text{t}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left( \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ for }\!\!~\!\!\text{ a }\!\!~\!\!\text{ metal, }\!\!~\!\!\text{ K}=\infty \right)$

(III) Consider a capacitor with two dielectric slabs of same thickness d placed inside it as shown. The slabs have dielectric constant ${{\text{k}}_{1}}\And {{\text{k}}_{2}}$ and areas ${{\text{A}}_{1}}\And \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{A}}_{2}}$ respectively. Treating the combination as two capacitors in parallel,

$\begin{array}{*{35}{r}} {} & C={{C}_{1}}+{{C}_{2}} \\ {} & C=\frac{{{k}_{1}}{{\in }_{0}}{{A}_{1}}}{d}+\frac{{{k}_{2}}{{\in }_{0}}{{A}_{2}}}{d} \\ {} & C=\frac{{{\in }_{0}}}{d}\left[ {{k}_{1}}{{A}_{1}}+{{k}_{2}}{{A}_{2}} \right] \\ \end{array}$
